title: Crepidomanes fallax
intro: Crepidomanes fallax is a species of fern, specifically a type of epiphytic filmy fern, known for its delicate and lacy fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes fallax include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Crepidomanes fallax is traditionally prepared in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to make tonics and other herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Crepidomanes fallax is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. The Hymenophyllaceae family is further divided into several genera, with Crepidomanes being one of them. The classification of Crepidomanes fallax places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
